component_identification.content:
#### Cards
- **112 character cards** — Each card belongs to one of 14 Colors (Gold, Silver, Copper, Red, Blue, Yellow, Green, Orange, Gray, Brown, White, Pink, Obsidian, Violet). Each card has a name, Color, deploy bonus, and end-game scoring condition.

#### Boards & Locations
- **1 game board** — Features 4 locations arranged in a row: Jupiter, Mars, Luna, and The Institute. Each location has a face-up row of cards and a specific deploy bonus.

#### Tracks & Tokens
- **1 Fleet track** — Printed on the board, numbered 0-7+. Players advance their marker when deploying to Jupiter.
- **Fleet markers** — 1 per player, placed on the Fleet track.
- **1 Sovereign token** — A large token worth end-game points. Gained by deploying to Luna; can change hands throughout the game.
- **Influence cubes** — Small cubes (1 set per player color). Placed on The Institute when deploying there.

#### Resources
- **Helium tokens** — Universal resource used for card abilities, end-game scoring, and certain deploy bonuses. Stored in a general supply.

#### Player Components
- **Player reference cards** — 1 per player, summarizing turn actions and end-game triggers.
- **Rising token** — Used to mark the first player each round.

#### Supply Reminders
- **Starting hand** — Each player begins with 5 cards in hand plus 2 Helium.
- **Location setup** — Place 1 face-up card from the deck at each of the 4 locations during setup.
- **Sovereign start** — The Sovereign token begins unclaimed beside the board.

core_game_loop.content:
#### Turn Overview
On your turn, you must do exactly one of two actions: **Lead** or **Scout**.

#### Action 1: Lead
1. **Deploy a card** — Choose 1 card from your hand and place it face-up at one of the 4 locations (Jupiter, Mars, Luna, or The Institute).
2. **Gain the location bonus** — Immediately resolve the bonus associated with that location:
   - **Jupiter** — Advance your Fleet marker 1 space on the Fleet track.
   - **Mars** — Draw 1 card from the top of the deck and add it to your hand. Then you may also take 1 Helium from the supply.
   - **Luna** — Take the Sovereign token (if you do not already have it). If no one has it, take it from beside the board.
   - **The Institute** — Place 1 of your Influence cubes on The Institute board space.
3. **Collect a card** — After gaining the location bonus, you MUST pick up all cards from a DIFFERENT location (not the one you just deployed to) and add them to your hand.

#### Action 2: Scout
1. **Draw from a location** — Pick up the top card from any one of the 4 locations and add it to your hand.
2. **OR draw from the deck** — Draw the top card of the deck instead.
3. **No deploy** — You do not play a card or gain a location bonus when scouting.

#### The Core Loop
The game is about cycling cards through your hand to build the highest-scoring combination. You deploy cards you do not need (gaining location bonuses in the process) and collect cards that synergize with your hand. Every Lead action forces you to give up a card but lets you gain both a location bonus AND cards from another location. Scouting is safer but slower, adding just one card without advancing your board presence.

#### Hand Management
- There is no hand size limit.
- Your final hand at game end IS your score — every card you hold contributes (or fails to contribute) to your total.
- Cards score based on printed base values plus conditional combo bonuses (e.g., "Worth 10 extra points if you also hold a Gold card").

detailed_rules.content:
#### The 14 Colors
Cards belong to one of 14 Colors from the Red Rising universe. Each Color has a thematic identity that loosely corresponds to its card abilities:
- **Gold** — High base values and powerful scoring conditions. Often reward having other Golds.
- **Silver** — Helium-focused. Many Silver cards grant or multiply Helium.
- **Copper** — Fleet-focused. Bonuses tied to Fleet track position.
- **Red** — Combo enablers. Often reward holding cards of many different Colors.
- **Blue** — Influence-focused. Synergize with cubes at The Institute.
- **Yellow** — Flexible utility cards with moderate base values.
- **Green** — Set-collection bonuses. Reward holding multiple Greens.
- **Orange** — Interaction cards. May force opponents to discard or swap.
- **Gray** — Military-themed. Often tied to Fleet track bonuses.
- **Brown** — Worker-themed. Moderate values with straightforward conditions.
- **White** — Sovereign-focused. Extra points if you hold the Sovereign token.
- **Pink** — Social cards. Reward having cards from specific Colors.
- **Obsidian** — High-risk, high-reward. Large bonuses with strict conditions.
- **Violet** — Political cards. Tied to Influence cubes and Sovereign.

#### Location Details
Each location accumulates cards over the course of the game as players deploy to them. When you Lead and collect from a different location, you take ALL cards currently at that location.

- **Jupiter** — Deploy here to advance Fleet. Collecting from Jupiter can yield multiple cards if several have been deployed here.
- **Mars** — Deploy here to draw a card from the deck plus optionally gain 1 Helium. Mars is the primary resource-generation location.
- **Luna** — Deploy here to claim the Sovereign token. Only one player holds the Sovereign at a time; deploying to Luna steals it.
- **The Institute** — Deploy here to place an Influence cube. Influence cubes are permanent once placed and count for end-game scoring.

#### The Sovereign Token
- Only 1 Sovereign token exists. It is claimed by deploying to Luna.
- If you already hold the Sovereign, deploying to Luna still grants the location bonus (you keep it).
- The Sovereign is worth bonus points at end of game (typically 5-10 points depending on game state).
- Some White and Violet cards have scoring conditions that reference the Sovereign.

#### The Fleet Track
- The Fleet track runs from 0 to 7+.
- You advance 1 space each time you deploy to Jupiter.
- Some card abilities also advance Fleet.
- Your Fleet position contributes directly to your end-game score (each space is worth approximately 2 points).
- Certain Copper and Gray cards grant bonus points based on Fleet position.

#### Influence Cubes
- Each player has a limited supply of Influence cubes.
- You place 1 cube at The Institute each time you deploy there.
- Cubes are permanent — once placed, they remain for the rest of the game.
- Each Influence cube at The Institute contributes to your end-game score.
- Blue and Violet cards often have conditions referencing how many Influence cubes you have placed.

#### Helium
- Helium is the game's universal resource, gained primarily from Mars.
- Some card abilities consume Helium for powerful effects.
- Unspent Helium at game end is worth points (1 point per Helium).
- There is no limit to how much Helium you can hold.

#### Banishing
- Certain card abilities allow you to **banish** cards — remove them from the game entirely (not to the discard pile).
- Banished cards go to a face-down banished pile and cannot be recovered.
- Some end-game triggers reference the number of banished cards.

#### Card Abilities
- Many cards have a **deploy bonus** that triggers when you play that card to a location (in addition to the location's own bonus).
- Deploy bonuses may include: draw extra cards, gain Helium, advance Fleet, place Influence, or banish cards from locations.
- Some cards have passive or end-game-only text (scoring conditions) with no deploy bonus.

scoring_and_endgame.content:
#### End-Game Triggers
The game end is triggered when ANY ONE of the following conditions is met at the end of a player's turn:
1. **Fleet threshold** — Any player's Fleet marker reaches space 7 on the Fleet track.
2. **Influence threshold** — Any player has placed their 7th Influence cube at The Institute.
3. **Banish threshold** — A total of 7 or more cards have been banished across all players combined.

When a trigger condition is met, each other player gets exactly 1 more turn. Then the game ends and scoring begins.

#### Scoring Categories
Players total their score from the following sources:

1. **Card base values** — Each card in your hand has a printed point value (typically 1-10). Sum the base values of all cards in your hand.

2. **Card combo bonuses** — Many cards have conditional scoring text (e.g., "Worth 8 bonus points if you hold 3 or more Red cards," or "Worth 5 bonus points for each Gold in your hand"). Evaluate every card's condition and add all earned bonuses.

3. **Fleet position** — Score points equal to your position on the Fleet track multiplied by 2. For example, Fleet position 5 = 10 points.

4. **Influence cubes** — Score 3 points for each Influence cube you have placed at The Institute.

5. **Sovereign bonus** — The player holding the Sovereign token at game end scores 7 bonus points.

6. **Helium** — Score 1 point for each Helium token you have remaining.

#### Final Scoring
Total = Card base values + Card combo bonuses + (Fleet position x 2) + (Influence cubes x 3) + Sovereign bonus (7 if held) + Helium remaining.

#### Winner and Tiebreakers
- **Winner** — The player with the highest total score wins.
- **Tiebreaker 1** — Most cards in hand.
- **Tiebreaker 2** — Most Helium remaining.
- **Tiebreaker 3** — Shared victory (extremely rare).

beginner_strategy.content:
#### Opening Priorities
- **Focus on card synergy, not location bonuses.** Location bonuses are valuable, but your hand of cards IS your score. Every deploy decision should ask: does this card help my final hand, or am I better off deploying it for a bonus and collecting something better?
- **Draft toward 2-3 Colors.** Spreading across too many Colors dilutes your combo potential. Pick 2-3 Colors early and build depth in those.
- **Do not ignore the Fleet track.** Even if you are not building a Copper/Gray fleet strategy, a few Jupiter deployments for Fleet advancement add reliable end-game points that do not depend on card combos.
- **Claim the Sovereign early if it is cheap.** Deploying to Luna when no one holds the Sovereign is essentially free points. The 7-point bonus is significant.
- **Scout when the locations are thin.** If no location has cards you want, Scouting from the deck is better than deploying a good card just to pick up mediocre ones.

#### Common Beginner Mistakes
- **Hoarding too many cards** — A large hand is only good if the cards combo. Dead cards in your hand are wasted base values with no combo bonus.
- **Deploying high-value cards carelessly** — Deploying a 9-point Gold to Jupiter for 1 Fleet advancement is often a bad trade. Deploy low-value cards and keep your high-value scorers.
- **Ignoring what opponents collect** — When you deploy a card to a location, you are feeding that card to whoever collects from there next. Avoid giving opponents cards that obviously fit their strategy.
- **Neglecting Influence cubes** — At 3 points each, Influence cubes are efficient and permanent. A few early Institute deployments provide a reliable scoring floor.
- **Chasing the Sovereign too aggressively** — Deploying to Luna repeatedly to hold the Sovereign is only worth it if you are not sacrificing better card-combo turns. The Sovereign is 7 points — a single strong card combo can be worth more.
- **Triggering the end too early** — Reaching a threshold (Fleet 7, 7 cubes, 7 banished) ends the game for everyone. Make sure your hand is ready before you trigger the ending, or you may hand the win to an opponent with a more complete combo set.

#### Strategic Principles
- **Lead is almost always better than Scout.** Leading lets you gain a location bonus AND collect cards. Scouting adds only 1 card. Default to Leading unless your hand is already strong or the board state is poor.
- **Read the Colors in your hand.** After 3-4 turns, commit to a Color strategy. If you have 2 Golds and a White that rewards Golds, lean into Gold. If you have Blues and Influence cubes, double down on Institute deployments.
- **Watch the end-game triggers.** Track Fleet positions, Influence cube counts, and the banish pile. The game can end suddenly, and the player who controls the timing has a major advantage.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Triggers
The game end is triggered when ANY ONE of the following conditions is met at the end of a player's turn:
1. **Fleet threshold** — Any player's Fleet marker reaches space 7 on the Fleet track.
2. **Influence threshold** — Any player has placed their 7th Influence cube at The Institute.
3. **Banish threshold** — A total of 7 or more cards have been banished across all players combined.

When a trigger condition is met, each other player gets exactly 1 more turn. Then the game ends and scoring begins.

#### Scoring Categories
Players total their score from the following sources:

1. **Card base values** — Each card in your hand has a printed point value (typically 1-10). Sum the base values of all cards in your hand.

2. **Card combo bonuses** — Many cards have conditional scoring text (e.g., "Worth 8 bonus points if you hold 3 or more Red cards," or "Worth 5 bonus points for each Gold in your hand"). Evaluate every card's condition and add all earned bonuses.

3. **Fleet position** — Score points equal to your position on the Fleet track multiplied by 2. For example, Fleet position 5 = 10 points.

4. **Influence cubes** — Score 3 points for each Influence cube you have placed at The Institute.

5. **Sovereign bonus** — The player holding the Sovereign token at game end scores 7 bonus points.

6. **Helium** — Score 1 point for each Helium token you have remaining.

#### Final Scoring
Total = Card base values + Card combo bonuses + (Fleet position x 2) + (Influence cubes x 3) + Sovereign bonus (7 if held) + Helium remaining.

#### Winner and Tiebreakers
- **Winner** — The player with the highest total score wins.
- **Tiebreaker 1** — Most cards in hand.
- **Tiebreaker 2** — Most Helium remaining.
- **Tiebreaker 3** — Shared victory (extremely rare).
